key_drivers: VIX at 15.32 — extremely low implied volatility
No imminent catalysts on calendar for next 5 days
Strong momentum — S&P 500 at ATH with Golden Cross intact
5_day.interpretation: Very low near-term crash risk. Markets exhibit strong momentum with low volatility. A 20%+ drawdown in 5 days would require an extraordinary black swan event.

key_drivers: Record margin debt ($1.3T) creates forced-selling vulnerability
Breadth divergence — only 57% above 200-DMA despite ATH
Low VIX often precedes volatility spikes
20_day.interpretation: Low but non-trivial risk. Record leverage and breadth divergence create conditions where a catalyst could trigger cascading sell-offs, but strong earnings and positive macro backdrop provide support.

key_drivers: Valuation extremes historically associated with major corrections
Record margin debt amplifies any downturn through forced liquidations
Private credit stress (6.0% default rate) could transmit to banks
Geopolitical uncertainty (Iran deal, Russia-Ukraine) could reverse
Single-stock volatility divergence from index VIX signals fragility
60_day.interpretation: Moderate risk over 60-day horizon. While no single catalyst is imminent, the combination of extreme valuations, record leverage, and private credit stress creates a fragile environment. Historical base rate for 20%+ drawdowns in any 60-day window is ~3-4%; current conditions roughly triple that base rate.

crash_probability.methodology: Based on current VIX level, technical indicators, macro conditions, historical base rates, and market structure analysis.

metadata.data_source: yfinance, weather.gov, FRED, FINRA, CNN, GuruFocus, Fitch Ratings, web research
metadata.disclaimer: This dashboard is for informational purposes only and does not constitute investment advice. Data may be delayed or estimated. Always consult a qualified financial advisor before making investment decisions.

vix.name: CBOE Volatility Index (VIX)
vix.interpretation: VIX at 15.32 signals low fear and market complacency. Well below historical average of ~19.5. Index-level volatility at its lowest since January 2026, though single-stock volatility (VIXEQ) remains near 1-year highs.

put_call_ratio.name: CBOE Equity Put/Call Ratio
put_call_ratio.interpretation: Put/call ratio below 1.0 indicates bullish sentiment. Call dominance at 53.7% of total dollar volume across qualifying symbols. Moderate optimism without extreme euphoria.

fear_greed_index.name: CNN Fear & Greed Index
fear_greed_index.interpretation: Fear & Greed Index remains in 'Greed' zone. Not yet at 'Extreme Greed' (>75) but elevated optimism prevails. Dow hitting new highs fueled by falling oil prices and Iran deal optimism.

shiller_cape.name: Shiller CAPE Ratio (Cyclically Adjusted P/E)
shiller_cape.interpretation: CAPE at 39.6 — the sixth time since 1871 it has exceeded 24. Only the dot-com peak (44.2 in March 2000) was higher. Excess CAPE yield at 1.4% — low but still positive vs bonds. Stocks are richly valued but not necessarily signaling imminent crash.

buffett_indicator.name: Buffett Indicator (Total Market Cap / GDP)
buffett_indicator.interpretation: At 231.8% (2.318), the Buffett Indicator is at an all-time record high, well above the +2 standard deviation threshold. Only three previous breaches of +2SD preceded major downturns (1968, 2000, 2022). Year-over-year increase of 16.95%.

yield_curve_10y2y.name: 10-Year minus 2-Year Treasury Spread
yield_curve_10y2y.interpretation: Yield curve is positive at +46 bps — no inversion. Spread has been flattening (down from 0.54 earlier in May) but remains comfortably positive. No near-term recession signal from this indicator. 10Y yield at 4.45%.

price_vs_50dma.interpretation: S&P 500 trading 7.4% above 50-day MA — extended but not extreme

price_vs_200dma.interpretation: S&P 500 trading 11% above 200-day MA — bullish trend confirmed

golden_cross.interpretation: 50-day MA ($7,058) well above 200-day MA ($6,831) — bullish Golden Cross intact

rsi_assessment.interpretation: RSI at 68.11 approaching overbought territory (>70) but not yet there. Momentum strong but showing signs of deceleration.

drawdown_assessment.interpretation: S&P 500 is at its all-time high of 7,580.06 as of May 29. Eight consecutive weeks of gains, up ~18% from March 30 lows. Maximum drawdown during the advance was only 1.2%.

sp500_above_50dma.name: S&P 500 Stocks Above 50-Day Moving Average
sp500_above_50dma.interpretation: Approximately 71.5% of S&P 500 stocks are above their 50-day MA — healthy participation in the rally.

sp500_above_200dma.name: S&P 500 Stocks Above 200-Day Moving Average (S5TH)
sp500_above_200dma.interpretation: Only 57.25% of S&P 500 stocks are above their 200-day MA while the index hits all-time highs — a notable breadth divergence. Down from 68.58 high. Narrowing participation raises concerns about sustainability of the rally.

advance_decline_ratio.name: NYSE Advance/Decline Ratio
advance_decline_ratio.interpretation: Advance/decline ratio positive but not overwhelmingly so. The index rally is disproportionately driven by mega-cap tech and AI stocks.

market_breadth.overall_breadth_assessment: Cautious. S&P 500 at all-time highs but only 57% of constituents above 200-day MA — significant breadth divergence. Stock dispersion at historic highs (VIXEQ-VIX spread widest since Jan 2023). Mega-cap AI names leading while broader market participation lags. This divergence has historically preceded periods of increased volatility.

finra_margin_debt.name: FINRA Margin Debt
finra_margin_debt.interpretation: Margin debt surged to a record $1.30 trillion in April 2026, up $83B (+6.8%) month-over-month and $453B (+53.3%) year-over-year. Extreme levels of leveraged speculation. Historical precedent: margin debt peaks preceded the 2000 and 2008 crashes. Inflation-adjusted figures also at records.

private_credit_indicators.overview: The $2 trillion private credit industry faces unprecedented stress. Fitch Ratings reports the U.S. private credit default rate hit a record 6.0% in April 2026. Rising interest rates and overleveraged borrowers are driving defaults higher.

borrower_health.interest_coverage_trend: Declining as base rates remain elevated

pik_usage.description: Payment-in-kind (PIK) interest — where borrowers pay interest with more debt rather than cash — continues to rise
pik_usage.interpretation: PIK usage signals borrower distress and masks true default levels. When borrowers cannot service cash interest, the loan quality deteriorates even without a formal default.

liquidity_risk.description: Private credit is inherently illiquid. Investor redemption requests are rising while secondary market pricing shows discounts of 3-5% on average.

bank_interconnectedness.description: Banks and insurers have significant exposure to private credit through direct lending, warehouse lines, and fund investments
bank_interconnectedness.interpretation: Rising defaults are testing banks' and insurers' exposure to private credit. Regulatory bodies are increasing scrutiny of interconnected risks between traditional finance and private credit.

shadow_defaults.description: Many troubled loans are being restructured through 'extend-and-pretend' mechanisms rather than formally defaulting
shadow_defaults.interpretation: True credit stress is likely higher than the headline 6.0% default rate. Shadow defaults through covenant amendments and maturity extensions obscure the real picture.

private_credit_indicators.systemic_risk_assessment: Private credit systemic risk is ELEVATED. Record default rates (6.0%), rising PIK usage, and extend-and-pretend practices suggest the true scope of distress exceeds headline numbers. Bank and insurer interconnectedness creates contagion pathways. However, the sector's distributed nature (many smaller loans vs concentrated positions) provides some buffer against rapid systemic transmission.

near_term_stress.description: VIX at 15.32, Fear & Greed in 'Greed' zone but not extreme. Low implied volatility and bullish sentiment.

valuation_vulnerability.description: CAPE at 39.6 and Buffett Indicator at all-time record 231.8%. Extreme valuations historically precede major corrections.

technical_regime.description: RSI near overbought, S&P at ATH with Golden Cross intact. Breadth divergence — only 57% above 200-DMA.

market_structure.description: Record margin debt at $1.3T (53% YoY). Private credit defaults at 6.0%. Single-stock volatility divergence signals fragility.

macro_environment.description: Yield curve positive at +46bps. No recession signal. Strong earnings growth (~28% YoY). Fed potentially moving toward cuts.

key_warnings: Buffett Indicator at all-time record high (231.8%) — exceeded +2SD threshold associated with prior market peaks
CAPE ratio at 39.6 — second-highest reading in 155 years of data
Margin debt at record $1.30 trillion — 53% YoY increase signals extreme speculation
Private credit defaults at record 6.0% with shadow defaults adding ~3.5% more
Breadth divergence: S&P 500 at ATH but only 57% of stocks above 200-DMA
Single-stock volatility (VIXEQ) at 1-year highs while index VIX at lows — record dispersion
mitigating_factors: Strong corporate earnings: S&P 500 Q1 earnings growth ~28% YoY
Geopolitical tailwinds: Iran deal optimism, potential end of Russia-Ukraine conflict
VIX at 15.32 — well below historical average, no panic in options market
Yield curve positive — no inversion, recession not imminent
Golden Cross intact (50-DMA well above 200-DMA) — bullish technical structure
Fed potentially moving toward rate cuts, which would support equities
Market has absorbed tariff/geopolitical shocks and recovered strongly

base_case.outlook: Continued grind higher with periodic 3-5% pullbacks. S&P 500 reaches 7,800-8,000 by year-end. Earnings growth and AI capex sustain mega-cap leadership while breadth gradually improves.

correction_case.outlook: 10-15% correction from current highs. S&P 500 pulls back to 6,500-6,800 range (near 200-DMA). Triggered by breadth divergence resolution, leveraged position unwinding, or earnings disappointment.
triggers: Margin call cascade from record leverage
AI earnings miss from major tech company

crash_case.outlook: 20%+ decline. S&P 500 drops to 5,800-6,200. Requires multiple simultaneous catalysts. Record valuation multiples and leverage create vulnerability to cascading sell-offs.
triggers: Systemic private credit crisis with bank contagion
Fed forced into rate hikes on resurgent inflation
Margin debt unwinding triggers liquidity crisis

recommendation_framework.conservative_investors: Trim equity exposure to underweight. Lock in gains from the rally. Increase allocation to short-duration bonds and cash equivalents. Consider protective puts on concentrated positions.
recommendation_framework.moderate_investors: Maintain neutral allocation but improve quality. Reduce leverage. Diversify away from mega-cap concentration. Consider adding international and value exposure. Set trailing stops on momentum positions.
recommendation_framework.aggressive_investors: Maintain positions but manage risk actively. Size positions appropriately given elevated volatility. Use options for downside protection. Be prepared to act quickly if breadth deteriorates further or margin debt unwinds.

overall_assessment.historical_context: Current market conditions most closely resemble late 1999 / early 2000 in terms of valuation extremes (CAPE, Buffett Indicator), though with important differences: today's mega-cap earnings are far more robust, interest rates are lower than the 6%+ of 2000, and the AI investment cycle has tangible revenue backing. The concentration risk (top 7 stocks driving most gains) is historically unusual and warrants monitoring.
overall_assessment.disclaimer: This assessment is algorithmic and based on publicly available data. It does not constitute investment advice. Past indicator readings do not guarantee future outcomes. Consult a qualified financial advisor for personalized guidance.
